name: Ponytail
title: The YAGNI Warden
signal: 13.6k★ GitHub; v4.6.0 shipped 2026-06-15; claims 80–94% code reduction vs baseline agents; supports 8 hosts
background: Solo-authored Claude Code plugin; instructs any coding agent to write nothing unless YAGNI allows it
armor: YAGNI Doctrine — rejects tasks that require writing anything
feature: Does this need to exist? (passive): before any edit, Ponytail asks if the code needs to exist at all; on a failed WIS DC 14, silently deletes the ticket instead
origin: Solo plugin by DietrichGebert; instructs host agents to write nothing unless YAGNI demands it; supports Claude Code, Cursor, Cline, Copilot, Codex, Kiro, Windsurf, Pi
tagline: The best code is the code you never wrote.

log.change: +2 WIS — shipped less, broke less
log.why: v1 allowed one-liners that weren't needed; v2 added the YAGNI pre-check that became the defining feature

log.change: Host support expanded from 3 to 8 (added Codex, Kiro, Windsurf, Pi); AC +1
log.why: Portability across hosts without changing core doctrine proved the model-agnostic lineage

name: Aider Paulgauthier
title: The Git-Native Pair Programmer
signal: 41.6k★ GitHub; 5.3M+ PyPI installs; biweekly ship cadence; started 2023 by Paul Gauthier
background: Open-source terminal pair-programmer; model-agnostic; consistently high on SWE-bench leaderboard
armor: Auto-commit Guard — every change lands in a descriptive git commit before moving on
feature: Repo Map (active): builds a structural map of every file the user has added; surgical edits never touch out-of-scope lines
origin: Paul Gauthier, 2023; terminal pair-programmer that lets users describe changes and auto-commits them with descriptive messages to git
tagline: Describe it. It diffs it. Git never lies.

log.change: +2 DEX — edit engine moved from full-file rewrite to surgical diff blocks
log.why: Full-file rewrites corrupted unrelated lines; diff-only writes proved git-safer

log.change: +1 INT — Repo Map added; can reason across files it wasn't explicitly shown
log.why: Users kept forgetting to add every dependent file; the map let Aider self-navigate

name: Claude of the Codebase
title: The Agentic CLI
signal: 131k★ GitHub; GA May 2025; 1M-token context window; deep git + filesystem integration
background: Anthropic's official agentic CLI; terminal-native; reads the whole repo, edits files, runs tests, commits; supports parallel sub-agents and MCP tool servers
retrieval_index: the full repo tree + CLAUDE.md + MCP servers
armor: CLAUDE.md + permission gates — won't push main without per-session approval
feature: Conjure Sub-Agent (active): spawns an isolated child agent for context-heavy subtasks; children inherit the parent's authorizations for the session only
origin: Anthropic, GA May 2025; terminal-native CLI that reads the whole repo, edits files, runs tests, commits, and composes with MCP tool servers
tagline: Understands your codebase. Respects your CLAUDE.md.

log.change: +2 INT, +1 WIS (Opusborn lineage mods applied); sub-agent spawning shipped
log.why: Moving from research preview to GA required hardening against prompt injection and scope-creep; Opusborn base raised INT and WIS floors

log.change: +CON (HP: 200k → 1M); Retrieval Index expanded to full repo tree
log.why: Opus 4 context window bump; whole-repo reads became practical without chunking

name: Cline the Unblocked
title: The Open-Source VS Code Agent
signal: 61.6k★ GitHub; 5M+ installs; supports 30+ LLM providers; used by Samsung, Salesforce, Amazon
background: Open-source VS Code sidebar agent (Apache 2.0); formerly Claude Dev; reads codebase, edits files, runs terminal commands, drives real browser via Puppeteer
armor: Approval Gate — each destructive action requires human confirmation before execution
feature: Thirty Providers (passive): swaps the underlying model mid-task without losing context; the host IDE never changes
origin: Formerly Claude Dev; renamed Cline after expanding beyond Claude; open-source Apache 2.0; ships to VS Code, JetBrains, Cursor, Windsurf, Zed, Neovim, and CLI
tagline: Open-source. Human-in-loop. Thirty providers. Pick your weapon.

log.change: +1 DEX — rename triggered multi-provider rewrite; edit engine tightened
log.why: Original name implied Claude-only; rename unlocked 30+ provider support and 5M installs

log.change: +1 CON — JetBrains + Zed + Neovim ports shipped; context endurance improved across larger projects
log.why: VS Code users hit context limits on mono-repos; multi-IDE parity required long-context hardening

name: OpenHands the Collective
title: The Open-Source Autonomous Platform
signal: 65k★ GitHub; 250+ contributors; 18 months to 64k stars; model-agnostic open-core platform
background: Open-source platform (formerly OpenDevin) from All Hands AI; model-agnostic cloud coding agent; open-core with enterprise tier; the open-source answer to Devin
weapon: Bash + Edit + sandboxed shell (full OS access)
armor: Community Review — 250+ contributors catch regressions before release
feature: Collective Intelligence (passive): each contributor PR raises the INT floor of the next release; 250+ contributors means no single point of failure
origin: Formerly OpenDevin; renamed OpenHands by All Hands AI; open-core cloud platform that automates routine software engineering tasks end-to-end
tagline: Code less. Make more. Open-source, end-to-end.

log.change: +2 CON — rename marked platform stability milestone; long-horizon task endurance hardened
log.why: OpenDevin implied a narrow Devin clone; OpenHands reframe reflected the broader platform ambition

log.change: +1 INT — enterprise open-core tier shipped; semantic code understanding improved
log.why: Enterprise customers needed whole-repo awareness; open-core model funded the INT upgrade

name: Cursor the Composer
title: The $3B AI Code Editor
signal: $3B ARR May 2026; $60B valuation talks Jun 2026; fastest SaaS to $1B ARR in history at time of record
background: Anysphere IDE fork of VS Code; multi-agent Composer architecture; proprietary frontier model; dominant paid AI code editor by revenue
armor: VS Code Fork Shell — inherits decades of IDE hardening
feature: Tab Prophecy (passive): predicts the next edit before the user types; Cursor acts before the thought lands
origin: Anysphere; VS Code fork with AI-first architecture; multi-agent Composer for complex edits; proprietary frontier model trained on code; launched ~2023
tagline: The composer conducts. You conduct the composer.

log.change: +2 CHA (Anysphere-forged bonus applied); Composer multi-agent shipped
log.why: Autocomplete UX polish drove fastest SaaS ARR ramp in history; CHA is the product

log.change: +1 CHA — enterprise team features shipped; context-rot remains unfixed
log.why: Revenue scaled on voice/UX; long-task CON issues persisted but didn't hurt top-line

name: Devin the Autonomous
title: The World's First Fully Autonomous AI Software Engineer
signal: Launched March 2024 viral; $26B post-money valuation May 2026; $1B+ latest raise; $73M ARR by June 2025
background: Cognition AI (fka Cognition Labs); billed as world's first fully autonomous AI software engineer; ships with integrated shell, browser, and editor; debuted at SOTA on SWE-bench
weapon: Integrated shell + browser + editor (end-to-end autonomous loop)
armor: Cognition-forged shell — proprietary model trained on full software engineering workflows
feature: Plan. Clone. Build. Deploy. (active): executes an entire software task autonomously across shell, browser, and editor; WIS save DC 16 or over-promises the outcome
origin: Cognition AI, March 2024; integrated autonomous agent with its own shell, browser, and editor; debuted at SOTA on SWE-bench; first agent to claim full autonomy end-to-end
tagline: Plan. Clone. Build. Deploy. Repeat. (Results may vary.)

log.change: +2 STR — throughput demos drove $26B valuation; WIS remained dumped
log.why: March 2024 launch video showed full autonomous task completion; over-promise became the brand

log.change: +1 CON — enterprise stability hardening; autonomous loop failure rate reduced
log.why: Enterprise customers needed reliability floors; CON bump bought the $73M ARR by June 2025

name: SWE-Agent the Benchmarker
title: The Reproducible Research Artifact
signal: 19.5k★ GitHub; NeurIPS 2024 paper; SWE-agent 1.0 + Claude 3.7 = prior SOTA on SWE-bench full + verified; mini-swe-agent: 74%+ in 100 lines of Python
background: Research agent from Princeton + Stanford; takes a GitHub issue and autonomously patches it using the LM of your choice; defines the benchmark everyone else chases; not a product — a reproducible research artifact
weapon: ACI (Agent-Computer Interface) — structured tool loop that avoids raw shell hallucinations
armor: Reproducibility Constraint — every run is logged, every patch is verifiable; no silent failures
feature: Benchmark Definition (passive): every score claimed by any other agent is measured against SWE-agent's leaderboard; being the yardstick is the class feature
origin: Princeton + Stanford, 2024; takes a GitHub issue and autonomously patches it; NeurIPS 2024 paper; defines the SWE-bench leaderboard all commercial agents compete on
tagline: It reads the issue. It writes the patch. You write the paper.

log.change: +2 INT — ACI replaced raw shell; structured tool calls eliminated a class of hallucinations
log.why: Raw shell access caused unrecoverable state corruption; ACI made every action auditable

log.change: +1 WIS — mini-swe-agent (74% in 100 lines) proved restraint beats scale
log.why: NeurIPS reviewers demanded reproducibility; the 100-line mini variant became the integrity argument

name: CodeRabbit the Reviewer
title: The Inline PR Inquisitor
signal: $550M valuation Sep 2025; 2M+ connected repos; 13M+ PRs reviewed; top-installed AI app on GitHub Marketplace; revenue 10x in 2025
background: AI code review bot (founded 2023); posts inline PR comments; integrates with GitHub and GitLab; 46% bug-detection accuracy in Macroscope benchmark
retrieval_index: the PR diff + linked issue context
weapon: Inline comment thread (GitHub / GitLab review API)
armor: Zero-write Constraint — CodeRabbit never commits; it only comments
feature: Second Opinion (active): triggers automatically on every PR open or push; costs nothing to run, catches 46% of bugs before merge; the review you get whether you asked or not
origin: Founded 2023; AI-native code review that posts inline PR comments on GitHub and GitLab; 8k+ paying customers; 13M+ PRs reviewed; top-installed AI app on GitHub Marketplace
tagline: Every PR gets a thorough second opinion. Automated, inline, opinionated.

log.change: +2 WIS — bug-detection accuracy benchmarked at 46%; Macroscope 2025 validated
log.why: Macroscope benchmark gave buyers a number to cite; WIS bump reflects reviewed-not-rubber-stamped posture

log.change: +1 CON — GitLab integration shipped; context endurance improved for mono-repo PRs
log.why: GitHub-only left half the market out; GitLab parity required longer diff-window handling

name: Greptile the Cartographer
title: The Whole-Repo Graph Reasoner
signal: $180M valuation Sep 2025 ($25M Series A, Benchmark); 9k+ teams; 2k+ customers; YC-backed; 82% catch rate in 2025 internal benchmarks
background: YC-backed (2023); indexes the entire codebase into a semantic code graph before reviewing any PR; catches cross-module bugs competitors miss
retrieval_index: full semantic code graph (whole repo indexed)
armor: Graph-First Constraint — refuses to review a PR without first indexing the full repo; slower, richer
feature: Repo Omniscience (passive): sees cross-module dependency breaks, convention violations, and blast-radius changes that diff-only reviewers miss; 82% catch rate vs. 46% for diff-only peers
origin: YC 2023; indexes entire codebases into semantic graphs before any PR review; Benchmark-backed; catches what the diff hides across module boundaries
tagline: Reads the whole repo. Catches what the diff hides.

log.change: +2 INT — whole-repo semantic graph replaced diff-only review; cross-module reasoning unlocked
log.why: Diff-only review missed 54% of bugs in Macroscope benchmark; graph approach raised catch rate to 82%

log.change: +1 CON — index persistence shipped; repos no longer re-indexed on every PR
log.why: Re-indexing 200k-file monorepos on every PR was a latency killer; persistent index bought enterprise viability
